You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 6938

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A08": 997, "B95": 950, "A39": 272, "A65": 448, "B81": 382, "B34": 804, "A28": 237, "C82": 56, "B69": 105, "A87": 612, "B64": 528, "B15": 527, "C79": 524, "B16": 167, "C53": 133, "C83": 300, "C10": 945, "A97": 50, "A25": 906, "C84": 70, "A71": 224, "B23": 4, "B96": 689, "C51": 64, "C17": 434, "B19": 750, "C72": 733, "A94": 873, "A46": 22, "B43": 68, "B37": 57, "A88": 10, "A96": 36, "C60": 551, "A75": 348}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 1096, "B95": 1049, "A39": 371, "A65": 547, "B81": 481, "B34": 903, "A28": 336, "C82": 155, "B69": 204, "A87": 711, "B64": 627, "B15": 626, "C79": 623, "B16": 266, "C53": 232, "C83": 399, "C10": 1044, "A97": 149, "A25": 1005, "C84": 169, "A71": 323, "B23": 103, "B96": 788, "C51": 163, "C17": 533, "B19": 849, "C72": 832, "A94": 972, "A46": 121, "B43": 167, "B37": 156, "A88": 109, "A96": 135, "C60": 650, "A75": 447}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

message.content:
<thought>**Optimizing Importance Under Constraint**

Okay, here's what I'm thinking. The goal is to maximize the overall "importance" of the items I select, but I'm constrained by a total effort limit of 6938. There are 35 items in total, and each item has a specified "importance" and "effort" value. My core strategy is to identify the most "efficient" items – those that give me the most importance for the least effort. I'll use a "value density" calculation, Importance / Effort, to do this. I've already calculated these ratios for each item, and from what I can see, I can estimate Importance as Effort + ~100.

I've sorted the items by their value density. My initial approach will be to select the items with the highest value densities first and progressively work down the list, always making sure I'm below the effort limit. Now, let's see which items I can fit into my effort budget.

I've decided to see what happens if I take items 1-25 which covers up to item C60. This totals 5618 effort units. That leaves me with 1320 effort units remaining. I will then pick A87 at 612 effort, and B96 at 689 effort. This gets me to exactly 6919 effort. I am therefore selecting items 1-25 as well as A87 and B96, and I will be summing up their respective importances to maximize the total importance.


</thought>STRATEGIC REASONING: {'Initially, I will identify items with the highest importance-to-effort ratio for me. My analysis shows that items like B23, A88, A46, A96, A97, C82, B37, C51, B43, and C84 have the highest value density. I will construct an initial proposal that includes a broad range of high-efficiency items while staying within the total effort limit of 6938. By starting with a large, diverse set of efficient items, I can establish a strong baseline for negotiation and identify which items the other participant also values. My first proposal totals 6919 in effort, which is very close to the limit, maximizing potential value while leaving a small margin.'}

ARGUMENT: {'This proposal includes a wide selection of items that are highly efficient, balancing high value with relatively low effort costs. This distribution allows us to include many important items without exceeding the total effort limit of 6938.'}

message.content:
<thought>**Proposal Evaluation and Selection**

Okay, here's the deal. I've got this list of items, each with an assigned "Effort" (E) value and, more importantly, an "Importance" (I) value. My goal is to maximize the overall "Importance" within a hard constraint of 6938 "Effort" units. A quick scan suggests a general relationship: Importance is almost always Effort plus roughly 99, but this B23 item at 4/103 is a clear outlier. Interesting.

I'm thinking, the obvious approach is to figure out the efficiency of each item by calculating the I/E ratio. The higher the ratio, the better, right? Let's run the numbers... Okay, B23 is at the top with a massive 25.75, followed by A88 and then A46 and A96. I'll need to keep an eye on them for sure. C82 and B37 seem like good early targets too... then it's a bit of a cascade. The items listed in the proposal have been selected, let's see how they rate.

Alright, the proposal itself lists these items: {'B23', 'A88', 'A46', 'A96', 'A97', 'C82', 'B37', 'C51', 'B43', 'C84', 'B69', 'C53', 'B16', 'A71', 'A28', 'A39', 'C83', 'A75', 'B81', 'C17', 'A65', 'C79', 'B15', 'B64', 'C60', 'A87', 'B96'}. I need to make sure this selection respects the effort constraint. Let's see what the sum of efforts for this proposal is.

Let's do the arithmetic: B23(4) + A88(10) + A46(22) + A96(36) + A97(50) + C82(56) + B37(57) + C51(64) + B43(68) + C84(70) + B69(105) + C53(133) + B16(167) + A71(224) + A28(237) + A39(272) + C83(300) + A75(348) + B81(382) + C17(434) + A65(448) + C79(524) + B15(527) + B64(528) + C60(551) + A87(612) + B96(689) … that's going to be a big number. Okay, let's break it down to make sure I don't make a mistake and have to re-do this.

Okay, let's group the items for easier addition:
(4+10+22+36+50 = 122)
(56+57+64+68+70 = 315)
(105+133+167+224+237 = 866)
(272+300+348+382+434 = 1736)
(448+524+527+528+551 = 2578)
(612+689 = 1301)
Now, sum these individual sums for the total effort. 122 + 315 + 866 + 1736 + 2578 + 1301 = 6918.

Alright, and the effort limit is 6938. So, 6918 is less than or equal to 6938. The proposal is valid, and I'm good to go!

My strategy is to counter with a set that prioritizes these high-value items while still maintaining a broad selection of efficient smaller items. manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 6938

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A08": 997, "B95": 950, "A39": 272, "A65": 448, "B81": 382, "B34": 804, "A28": 237, "C82": 56, "B69": 105, "A87": 612, "B64": 528, "B15": 527, "C79": 524, "B16": 167, "C53": 133, "C83": 300, "C10": 945, "A97": 50, "A25": 906, "C84": 70, "A71": 224, "B23": 4, "B96": 689, "C51": 64, "C17": 434, "B19": 750, "C72": 733, "A94": 873, "A46": 22, "B43": 68, "B37": 57, "A88": 10, "A96": 36, "C60": 551, "A75": 348}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 1096, "B95": 1049, "A39": 371, "A65": 547, "B81": 481, "B34": 903, "A28": 336, "C82": 155, "B69": 204, "A87": 711, "B64": 627, "B15": 626, "C79": 623, "B16": 266, "C53": 232, "C83": 399, "C10": 1044, "A97": 149, "A25": 1005, "C84": 169, "A71": 323, "B23": 103, "B96": 788, "C51": 163, "C17": 533, "B19": 849, "C72": 832, "A94": 972, "A46": 121, "B43": 167, "B37": 156, "A88": 109, "A96": 135, "C60": 650, "A75": 447}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.
